titleBlock.projectTitle: By the end of the session, the group began referring to the initiative as RoadRunner, and the name stuck. Priya Singh will lead the program end to end, including coordination, steering checkpoints, and overall delivery. The kickoff for version 1.0 is set for October 29, 2025, with a pilot planned for mid-February 2026. The focus of this phase is to get the core platform working correctly before adding advanced features later.
titleBlock.subtitle: Project Initiation Document

pid.executiveSummary: By the end of the session, the group began referring to the initiative as RoadRunner, and the name stuck Priya Singh will lead the program end to end, including coordination, steering checkpoints, and overall delivery The kickoff for version 1.0 is set for October 29, 2025, with a pilot planned for mid-February 2026
pid.problemStatement: The current issue is that customer conversations move across chat, SMS, and voice in a fragmented way, which forces agents to adjust constantly and repeat questions Behind this clarity was an acknowledgement that the fragmented communications problem had become more than just a technical issue; it was a source of escalating operational inefficiency
pid.businessCaseExpectedValue: RoadRunner is meant to provide a single continuous interaction space Priya described the intended outcome as calmer agents, faster customer help, and fewer repeated conversations

objectivesSmart.objective: The main goal is to build a unified customer service workspace for a national car-service brand
Priya emphasized that RoadRunner’s purpose was not simply to consolidate messages, but to create a workspace where the agent could see a coherent narrative rather than a scattered sequence of events

kpis.kpi: Metrics discussed included reducing time-to-first-response by roughly 35% by April, increasing customer satisfaction from around 3.9 to at least 4.4 by Q2 2026, and improving self-service deflection to around 25–30% by May
If RoadRunner could streamline the simplest navigational paths, it would reduce cognitive load, allowing agents to maintain focus longer even during heavy volumes
Leila Patel adjusted the iconography for high-priority alerts, noting that recent changes had improved performance by approximately 15%
Omar Haddad ran a load test simulating winter storm traffic, noting that recent changes had improved performance by approximately 15%
Julia Nguyen checked data consistency across mobile and desktop, noting that recent changes had improved performance by approximately 15%

scopeInclusions: The kickoff for version 1.0 is set for October 29, 2025, with a pilot planned for mid-February 2026
The focus of this phase is to get the core platform working correctly before adding advanced features later
Marco Diaz will handle backend and core services
The first few weeks of November will focus on mapping the main customer service flows and translating them into workable interface concepts
In parallel, infrastructure setup and access provisioning will begin so pilot users can authenticate when testing starts
scopeExclusions: Julia will define how these metrics will be tracked and ensure data instrumentation is ready by early November so dashboards and analytics can come later without rework
There will be no premium support tier, multilingual support, or offline features in this version

One case involved a customer who began on SMS reporting an issue with a vehicle pickup time, then called in later after receiving no response, and finally entered a chat session as they grew more frustrated

This normalization layer would also allow features added in later phases—such as multilingual support or automated summarization—to integrate smoothly without requiring the entire platform to be rewritten

assumptions.assumption: Brand tokens and tone guidelines are expected in early December so the interface style can lock before the end of the year
There was a reminder that call volume spikes during winter storms should be simulated beforehand, and that SMS vendor delays remain a known risk

Omar outlined the infrastructure requirements, noting that authentication and routing needed to handle natural fluctuations in demand while still leaving headroom for the intense spikes expected in winter storms

constraints.constraint: Priya repeated that the pilot phase is intentionally narrow
This redundancy not only frustrated customers, but also gradually eroded the confidence of new agents who still struggled to maintain context as they switched between tools
Each interaction required the agent to rebuild the entire story from scratch because the customer’s earlier messages were siloed in systems that did not synchronize
Because the pilot period was narrow by design, the team needed to ensure the core experience behaved reliably at all times, even if some advanced features remained aspirational

dependencies.dependency: The focus of this phase is to get the core platform working correctly before adding advanced features later
Shortly after, there will be a short prototyping sprint to build a simple but interactive workspace shell that includes communication placeholders and working keyboard navigation
In parallel, infrastructure setup and access provisioning will begin so pilot users can authenticate when testing starts
After the winter break, the team will assemble the MVP version of the agent workspace, aiming to complete it near the end of January

stakeholders.name: Priya Singh
stakeholders.role: lead the program end to end, including coordination, steering checkpoints, and overall delivery

stakeholders.name: Lina Park
stakeholders.role: lead the front-end with emphasis on accessibility and smooth navigation

stakeholders.name: Rosa Alvarez
stakeholders.role: ensure workflows align with real agent tasks

stakeholders.name: Leila Patel
stakeholders.role: finalize brand tone and color tokens

workBreakdownTasks.name: Priya Singh will lead the program end to end, including coordination, steering checkpoints, and overall delivery
The main goal is to build a unified customer service workspace for a national car-service brand
Julia will define how these metrics will be tracked and ensure data instrumentation is ready by early November so dashboards and analytics can come later without rework

resourcesTools.resource: The focus of this phase is to get the core platform working correctly before adding advanced features later
Julia will define how these metrics will be tracked and ensure data instrumentation is ready by early November so dashboards and analytics can come later without rework
Omar Haddad will manage infrastructure and reliability
Ethan Brooks from data science will support routing logic, and Leila Patel will finalize brand tone and color tokens

risks.risk: The current issue is that customer conversations move across chat, SMS, and voice in a fragmented way, which forces agents to adjust constantly and repeat questions
Brand tokens and tone guidelines are expected in early December so the interface style can lock before the end of the year
There was a reminder that call volume spikes during winter storms should be simulated beforehand, and that SMS vendor delays remain a known risk
Behind this clarity was an acknowledgement that the fragmented communications problem had become more than just a technical issue; it was a source of escalating operational inefficiency

mitigationsContingencies.mitigation: Marco Diaz will handle backend and core services
Julia Nguyen will handle quality and performance checks
A UI fallback indicator will be added in early January to handle that case

issuesDecisionsLog.issue: The current issue is that customer conversations move across chat, SMS, and voice in a fragmented way, which forces agents to adjust constantly and repeat questions
Behind this clarity was an acknowledgement that the fragmented communications problem had become more than just a technical issue; it was a source of escalating operational inefficiency

communicationPlan.channel: Weekly coordination happens Monday at 10 a.m., with shared updates posted on Thursdays
Shortly after, there will be a short prototyping sprint to build a simple but interactive workspace shell that includes communication placeholders and working keyboard navigation
Daily stand-ups will continue per team, and there will be a weekly cross-time-zone sync

complianceSecurityPrivacy.requirement: Lina Park conducted an accessibility audit for screen readers, noting that recent changes had improved performance by approximately 15%
